user_prompt: remove the libsbc from platformio.ini, as I have made a copy in `lib\sbc`, no code changes so far but verify what I did is valid
agent_reply: Your `lib\sbc` copy is valid for PlatformIO as-is: the build now detects it as a local project library named `sbc`, compiles it, and `#include "sbc.h"` resolves correctly.
One small note: PlatformIO is compiling the copied command-line/test files too:
They appear to compile into tiny object files and don’t break anything, but they aren’t needed for firmware. Later we may want a `library.json` or source filter to include only the codec files.

user_prompt: I removed references to WM8960 because it is obsolete, new hardware target is SGTL5000 but I renamed a ton of stuff as external I2S codec
agent_reply: If WM8960 is obsolete, this dependency should be removed or replaced with the SGTL5000/control library you actually plan to use. Right now the build still pulls WM8960-specific code.
That conflicts with the new direction. If WM8960 is no longer valid hardware, this doc should say only SGTL5000 or generic external I2S codec.

Both `enable_exti2scodec_speaker()` and `enable_exti2scodec_mic()` return `false`, so selecting `Hardware::ExternalI2SCodec` cannot currently enable speaker or mic mode. If the rename was meant to preserve behavior while swapping codec hardware, this is the main functional gap.
4. [AudioManager.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:553) assumes every external I2S codec mic stream is stereo.

That matches what you described, but it should be considered part of the external codec contract. If SGTL5000 is ever configured mono at the codec or I2S slot level, this path will incorrectly halve the frame count and mix adjacent mono samples as L/R.

The broad rename to `ExternalI2SCodec` looks consistent in `AudioManager.h/.cpp`; I don’t see leftover `WM8960` identifiers in that module. The stale references are mainly dependency/docs, plus the external codec setup stubs. I couldn’t use `git diff` because this shell reported the directory as not a git repo.

user_prompt: I want a project wide removal of SD and we are changing to using SdFat instead. VFS paths are not important. What is important is that the files being created have actual creation time stamp file attributes so we can sort them later.
agent_reply: Implemented the project-wide SD to SdFat move.
- Updated GUI image SD fallback to use SdFat instead of Arduino `SD`: [FlyGui.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp)

Timestamp handling is now centralized through `FsDateTime::setCallback(...)`, using `ClockAgent` to populate FAT creation/sync timestamps via `FS_DATE` / `FS_TIME`. I also removed the VFS path API and POSIX `truncate()` usage; files are now truncated through `FsFile::truncate()` while still open.
I verified there are no remaining `#include <SD.h>` or `SD.` references. I couldn’t run a PlatformIO build because `pio` is not installed/on PATH here, and `python -m platformio` is unavailable.

References used for SdFat timestamp/preallocation behavior: [SdFat GitHub](https://github.com/greiman/SdFat), [SdFat timestamp callback notes](https://github-wiki-see.page/m/renesas/Arduino/wiki/How-to-set-timestamps-on-files-handled-by-SdFat).
Fixed the SdFat warning by adding this build flag in [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:18):

That warning is emitted intentionally by SdFat when Arduino `FS.h` is available and SdFat chooses not to define its own `File` typedef. Since `SdFat.h` is included from multiple places, the build flag is the cleanest project-wide fix.
Verification: `pio run` completed successfully, and the `File not defined because __has_include(FS.h)` warning is gone. There are still other unrelated warnings in the build output.
Fixed the SdFat warning by adding this build flag in [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:18):

That warning is emitted intentionally by SdFat when Arduino `FS.h` is available and SdFat chooses not to define its own `File` typedef. Since `SdFat.h` is included from multiple places, the build flag is the cleanest project-wide fix.
Verification: `pio run` completed successfully, and the `File not defined because __has_include(FS.h)` warning is gone. There are still other unrelated warnings in the build output.

user_prompt: implement `src\tests\test_screen.cpp` according to the comments inside
agent_reply: - Creates a `FlyGui` and uses `gui.display()` for screen access.
- Loops forever through HSV hues at 100% saturation and 50% value.
- Reports FPS to Serial every 2 seconds.

I also had to update [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:18) with the Arduino `FS/src` include path. Pulling in `FlyGui` also pulls in `LittleFS`, and that library needed `FS.h` visible to compile.

user_prompt: first, look at `inc\version.h`, implement the strings in `src\version.c` using as much of compiler preprocessor definitions as possible.
`syncToCompileTime` needs to use `compiler_time_str`, if `compiler_time_str` is in the future, sync the physical RTC to it.
somehow make sure `version.c` is compiled every build, maybe something to do with `platformio.ini`, maybe adding a script to `tools`
it is acceptable if `src\version.c` is automatically generated by a tool, as long as `inc\version.h` stays consistent
do not touch other files not mentioned
agent_reply: - Added [tools/generate_version_source.py](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/generate_version_source.py), which regenerates `src/version.c` before every build so `__DATE__` / `__TIME__` are fresh.
- Updated [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) to run that generator as a pre-build script.
- If RTC is invalid, or compile time is newer, writes compile time to the physical RTC.
- Otherwise syncs the software clock state from the existing RTC.
Verification: `pio run` passed. I also ran a second build and confirmed PlatformIO recompiled `src/version.c.o` again, so the pre-build regeneration is doing its job.
- Added [tools/generate_version_source.py](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/generate_version_source.py), which regenerates `src/version.c` before every build so `__DATE__` / `__TIME__` are fresh.
- Updated [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) to run that generator as a pre-build script.
- If RTC is invalid, or compile time is newer, writes compile time to the physical RTC.
- Otherwise syncs the software clock state from the existing RTC.

commit_message: speakerphone test works, it did involve editing the esp-idf config and building the library in the back

user_prompt: fix the build, on this machine, it's fine on my home computer, use `[env:thefly]` to start
looks like a missing python package?
agent_reply: Fixed the local build path for `[env:thefly]`. It now builds successfully with:
- Added `lib_ignore = EspFileManager` in [platformio.ini](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:53), because that unused dependency was wedging this machine’s compiler.
- Finished the earlier `BtHostList::pruneBonds()` addition, and it compiles.
- removed the broken `.espidf-5.3.2` venv/cache and let PlatformIO recreate it
- patched pioarduino’s installed `espidf.py` for two local builder issues:
- `$ESPIDF_PYTHONEXE` being used before assignment, causing `"None"` to execute
Verification: `[env:thefly]` build passed. Final size: RAM `64.3%`, Flash `32.1%`.
Note: `src/version.c` regenerated during the build, as expected.

commit_message: iram freed up so now png drawing works because psram available

user_prompt: The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect.
build error, my home computer builds the project fine
recently we tweaked the sdkconfig stuff inside `platformio.ini` enough so that we have enough IRAM to start using PSRAM again, we did that because PNG decoding was failing to allocate enough heap.
agent_reply: Fixed. The build error was a malformed generated command: this machine’s PlatformIO ESP-IDF builder was producing `python.exepython.exe`, so Windows rejected it as an invalid filename.
- Added PSRAM/16 MB local board config: [boards/thefly-core2.json](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/boards/thefly-core2.json:1)
- Pointed `[env:thefly]` at that board and pinned IDF flash size to 16 MB: [platformio.ini](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:41)
- Added a small post-build env fix for duplicated `ESPIDF_PYTHONEXE`: [tools/fix_espidf_pythonexe.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/fix_espidf_pythonexe.py:1)
I also removed the ignored stale local `sdkconfig.thefly*` files so they regenerated from the corrected 16 MB defaults.

commit_message: progress on bluetooth but cannot initiate connection, can handle incoming though
build was bootlooping, got it fixed
test build to try fixing dev initiated bt connection

I made the next diagnostic change: BluetoothManager.cpp now initializes and returns closed Bluetooth state to ESP_BT_NON_CONNECTABLE / ESP_BT_NON_DISCOVERABLE, matching the old working code more closely. Pairing still opens discoverable mode.

This is deliberately a test build: passive host-initiated connection while idle may not work in this version. The point is to test whether ESP32-initiated HFP starts working when HFP is initialized from the old non-connectable idle mode.
starting to add a web server
moved the ftp library to my fork
recording works great now, tweaked the ui
attempt to make build more reliable

user_prompt: My Codex login has keys stored here "C:\Users\frank\.codex\auth.json", or otherwise it is supposed to be in `~/.codex/auth.json`
write a python script, `tools\check_dev_pace.py`, that checks the my current codex usage and report it against the limits.
if it fails, make it fail without an error return code, if used in a part of another script, it appears to succeed
add it as an end build script to `platformio.ini`
My Codex login has keys stored here "C:\Users\frank\.codex\auth.json", or otherwise it is supposed to be in `~/.codex/auth.json`
write a python script, `tools\check_dev_pace.py`, that checks the my current codex usage and report it against the limits.
if it fails, make it fail without an error return code, if used in a part of another script, it appears to succeed
add it as an end build script to `platformio.ini`
agent_reply: - Added [tools/check_dev_pace.py](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/check_dev_pace.py:1), a stdlib-only checker that reads Codex auth, requests Codex rate-limit headers, reports 5-hour/7-day usage percent, remaining percent, reset time, and exits `0` even on failures.
- Kept both touched files normalized to CRLF.
- Missing-auth failure path: printed a skip message and returned `exit=0`
I used the repo you pointed at for the auth/header approach: https://github.com/Maol-1997/codex-stats
Note: I did not run the live Codex request with your real auth during validation, to avoid spending a service request. Git also reports `src/version.c` modified; I did not touch that file.
